Functions for collecting and updating various analytics data from different system modules.

# Purpose
This C header file declares a set of functions related to analytics data collection in a system. The file includes the `inttypes.h` library and a custom header `drivers/rtc.h`, which suggests that it interacts with real-time clock functionalities. The function [`analytics_external_update`](<#analytics_external_update>) is defined to request updates to analytics fields from other modules. Additionally, the file declares several external functions for collecting various types of analytics data, such as battery statistics, CPU and flash read statistics, Bluetooth parameters, and system settings. These functions are likely implemented elsewhere and are intended to gather and update analytics data from different components of the system.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `inttypes.h`
- `drivers/rtc.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

### analytics\_external\_collect\_battery<!-- {{#callable_declaration:analytics_external_collect_battery}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/analytics/analytics_external.h#L25>)

Collects and updates battery analytics data.
- **Description**: Use this function to collect and update battery-related analytics data, including voltage and percentage changes. It should not be called within an hour after the system boots up to ensure accurate data collection. The function updates various battery metrics, assuming the device was not charging during the last hour. This function is part of a larger analytics system and interacts with other modules to update battery metrics.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`analytics_external_collect_battery`](<../battery/battery_state.c.md#analytics_external_collect_battery>)  (Implementation)


---
### analytics\_external\_collect\_cpu\_stats<!-- {{#callable_declaration:analytics_external_collect_cpu_stats}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/analytics/analytics_external.h#L29>)

Collects and updates CPU statistics for analytics.
- **Description**: Use this function to gather and update CPU-related metrics for analytics purposes. It calculates the CPU stop time, sleep time, and running time, and updates these metrics in the analytics system. This function should be called periodically to ensure that the analytics system has up-to-date information about CPU usage. It resets internal counters for sleep and stop times after updating the metrics.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`analytics_external_collect_cpu_stats`](<../../../freertos_application.c.md#analytics_external_collect_cpu_stats>)  (Implementation)


---
### analytics\_external\_collect\_stop\_inhibitor\_stats<!-- {{#callable_declaration:analytics_external_collect_stop_inhibitor_stats}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/analytics/analytics_external.h#L30>)

Collects analytics data for various system inhibitors.
- **Description**: Use this function to gather analytics data related to different system inhibitors at a specific point in time. It is important to provide the current time in ticks as an argument to ensure accurate data collection. This function is typically used in systems where monitoring of CPU usage and other resources is necessary to optimize performance and diagnose issues. Ensure that the system clock is correctly initialized before calling this function to avoid inaccurate data collection.
- **Inputs**:
    - `now_ticks`: Represents the current time in ticks. Must be a valid `RtcTicks` value. The caller must ensure that this value is accurate and reflects the current system time.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`analytics_external_collect_stop_inhibitor_stats`](<../../../kernel/util/stop.c.md#analytics_external_collect_stop_inhibitor_stats>)  (Implementation)


---

[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/analytics/analytics_external.h#L32>)

Collects and updates Bluetooth pairing information for analytics.
- **Description**: Use this function to gather the current count of Bluetooth Classic pairings and update the analytics system with this data. It is intended for use in systems where tracking Bluetooth pairing metrics is necessary. Ensure that the analytics system is initialized before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`analytics_external_collect_bt_pairing_info`](<../../normal/bluetooth/bluetooth_persistent_storage.c.md#analytics_external_collect_bt_pairing_info>)  (Implementation)

### analytics\_external\_collect\_ble\_parameters<!-- {{#callable_declaration:analytics_external_collect_ble_parameters}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/analytics/analytics_external.h#L33>)

Collects BLE parameters for analytics.
- **Description**: Use this function to gather Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) parameters for analytics purposes. It locks the Bluetooth subsystem to safely access BLE connection data and attempts to collect channel usage metrics. If successful, it updates the analytics system with the collected data. This function must be called when BLE analytics data is needed, and it assumes that the Bluetooth subsystem is initialized and operational. It does not return any value and has no parameters.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`analytics_external_collect_ble_parameters`](<../../../comm/bluetooth_analytics.c.md#analytics_external_collect_ble_parameters>)  (Implementation)

### analytics\_external\_collect\_ble\_pairing\_info<!-- {{#callable_declaration:analytics_external_collect_ble_pairing_info}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/analytics/analytics_external.h#L34>)

Collects and updates BLE pairing information for analytics.
- **Description**: Use this function to gather and update the number of BLE pairings and persistent storage updates for analytics purposes. It is intended to be called when there is a need to refresh the analytics data related to BLE pairings. The function resets the count of persistent storage updates after updating the analytics system. Ensure that the analytics system is initialized before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`analytics_external_collect_ble_pairing_info`](<../../normal/bluetooth/bluetooth_persistent_storage.c.md#analytics_external_collect_ble_pairing_info>)  (Implementation)

### analytics\_external\_collect\_stack\_free<!-- {{#callable_declaration:analytics_external_collect_stack_free}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/analytics/analytics_external.h#L42>)

Collects and updates stack free metrics for various system tasks.
- **Description**: Use this function to gather and update analytics data related to the available stack space for key system tasks. This function is useful for monitoring system resource usage and ensuring that tasks have sufficient stack space. It must be called when you need to update the stack free metrics for tasks such as the kernel main, kernel background, Bluetooth host, Bluetooth controller, Bluetooth HCI, and new timers. This function does not return any value and does not modify any input parameters.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`analytics_external_collect_stack_free`](<../../../kernel/pebble_tasks.c.md#analytics_external_collect_stack_free>)  (Implementation)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/analytics/analytics_external.h#L50>)

Collects and resets the count of received accelerometer samples for analytics.
- **Description**: Use this function to gather the current count of accelerometer samples that have been received and reset the count to zero. This function is typically called when you need to update analytics data with the number of accelerometer samples collected since the last reset. It ensures that the count is safely accessed and modified by using a mutex to prevent concurrent access issues.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`analytics_external_collect_accel_samples_received`](<../accel_manager.c.md#analytics_external_collect_accel_samples_received>)  (Implementation)
